place: Three Bridges CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Three Bridges earned its name from three historic bridges that spanned the South Branch Raritan River, with the final bridge built in 1813 and the name solidified by 1866.
The South Branch Raritan River runs through Three Bridges, shaping local hydrology and occasionally causing floods monitored by nearby USGS gauges.
Railroad history kicked off in 1854 when the Central Railroad of New Jersey opened its South Branch Line, turning the village into a transportation hub.
The town’s two NJ Transit stations on the Morristown Line connect residents directly to New York Penn Station, Secaucus Junction, and Newark Broad Street.
The Millburn station sits at Essex Street and Lackawanna Place beside the Millburn Free Public Library, while the Short Hills station lies near The Crescent Street.
